- Virtual private networks (VPNs): A VPN can help to secure your internet connection and protect your personal information when you are connecting to the internet from a public space, like a café or airport.

- Suspicious activity on online accounts associated with your credit card 
- Unsolicited emails or phone calls claiming to have information about your credit card 
- Changes in the way you receive statements for your credit card 
- Unexexplained charges for goods or services that you never intended to buy 
- Unexpected calls from debt collection agencies regarding a credit card account that you don't recognize 
- Suspicious activity on your bank accounts or other financial accounts linked to the credit card 
If you experience any of these signs of credit card fraud, contact your credit card issuer or bank immediately to report the suspicious activity. You can also take steps to increase your security, such as setting up two-factor authentication and using a secure password for your online accounts. Regularly monitoring your credit report and taking steps to protect your identity can also help to avoid credit card fraud.
